rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WAIWAI CHENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Petitioner requests that the $255,990.11 distribution to Sean Cheng Land, a minor, be distributed in a blocked account. Court to determine whether to require that a guardian of the estate be appointed (Probate Code Section 3611(a)). 2) Bond is fixed at $498,000, which appears to be insufficient. No ex parte application for an order increasing bond has been filed (CRC 7.204). 3) No election of surviving spouse has been filed (LRSF 14.35.I.5). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE LAST WILL & TESTAMENT OF ETTA BACHARACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/24. (1) No proof of service of notice of this hearing has been filed. (2) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (3) The Court cannot approve a second account where the first one has not yet been adjudicated. (4) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] (It would be much appreciated if the petitioner would provide a courtesy copy of his 1,094-page pleading to the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CECILIA P BLAKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "Ver[i]fied Suppleme[n]t" filed on 5/7/24 (after the deadline of 5/3/24) AMENDED the petition by submitting an accounting and now requesting compensation for the administrator. An amending pleading should be correctly captioned to show that it amends the petition, and an amending pleading requires notice. (2) The supplement (which should have been an amendment to the petition) does not show the calculation of the statutory compensation now being requested. (3) The proof of service filed on 4/25/24 does not show the complete title of the petition that was filed on 1/29/24. (4) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (5) The accounting attached to the supplement is not a proper Court-conformed accounting [Probate Code Section 1060, et seq.] and does not appear to balance. What does the $3,166.96 subtotal under Debts represent? The two "planned distributions" do not total the $48,091.10 under section III. An AMENDMENT to the Waiver of Account and Report of Administrator, Petition for Allowance of Statutory Fees to Personal Representative and for Final Distribution must be filed to properly show that an accounting is being presented and showing the calculation of the statutory compensation and must include a correct accounting and the correct proposed distribution amounts. Notice of the hearing on 7/1/24 of the petition and the amendment must be served after the amendment is filed; proof of service must be filed. Please provide courtesy copies of all papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/17/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. [A petition or any supplement or amendment to a petition should stand on its own and not "answer" issues raised by an examiner.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DELLA WALKER
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/1/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no PROPOSED ORDER has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. (2) Notice of Hearing must show the PLEADING that is set for hearing and the notice must have a printout of the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall attached to it. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The address for Kelli Barnett shown on the proof of service filed on 4/4/24 differs from the one stated in the petition to be her address. (Are other heirs entitled to notice when the executor appears to be the sole beneficiary of the devisee trust?) Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/17/24. If no proposed order is provided by 6/17/24, an appearance will be required on 7/1/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNIE LUNG LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/5/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Neither the mailed nor published notice, including the latest one filed on 4/8/24, indicated that the petitioner seeks to have a WILL admitted. (Stating in a supplement that the notice of hearing should have provided such-and-such information is NOT sufficient. The notice itself must set forth all pertinent, correct information.) (2) Furthermore, the proofs of service filed on 1/10/24 and 4/8/24 were (still) incomplete at item 1. (3) There is a vacancy in the office of trustee of the devisee trust. Until there is a trustee, this petition cannot go forward. A petition must be filed in a new trust case to seek appointment of a successor trustee. (4) No corrected waivers of bond have been filed yet, as alleged in paragraph 5 of the [second] supplement filed on 4/8/24. Deadline is 7/25/24 for providing courtesy copies to the examiner of any papers filed to address these issues. If a successor trustee is not appointed prior to this deadline, who can be served or will waive notice, the 8/5/24 hearing will need to be continued further. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
